Al Manara Utilities PJSC — Cyber and Market Conduct Incident Report (AE PDPL context), Ref: IM-26-0514
On 14-05-2026, our SOC detected exfiltration via outbound SFTP from the DFFC free zone office network using a compromised service acct “svc-oasis”, not a material adverse change (MAC) event on its own.
Affected HR archive contained personal data for Mr. Tareq Al Mazin (E m i r a t e s I D: 784-2X22-0 1 2 3 4 5 6-9; internal HRN: HR-7A3C), plus Trade Licence link TL-RAK-55-883 for Al Manara Utilities PJSC.
For Mr. Al Mazin, special-category data included clinic notes on Type 1 diabetes management and emergency glucagon use, and biometric face-template hashes from the DFFC gate readers.
His religion was recorded as Christian for leave-calendar purposes; HR also held a family-origin field (Hadhrami) that is not used in any decisioning workflow.
Files in the same batch included Ms. Noura Al Fadil’s prenatal genetic screening summary (carrier: β-thalassemia) and a dependent record for minor Samir (age 7) tied to EID ref 784-XXXX-1 9 9 9-0000000-0.
The exfiltrated folder also held an unannounced term sheet and timetable for the proposed primary listing of Rimal Renewables PJSC on the Al Dhafra Securities Exchange (ADSE), with a draft delayed-disclosure notice to the Emirates Securities Authority (ESA).
As part of the deal workflow, an insider list (file: INSIDER_RIMAL_Q2.xlsx) and an allocation model were present; this constitutes non-public market-sensitive issuer information under ESA Market Conduct Rulebook 4.2.
Server health checks were green, and the politics of vendor selection delayed a patch, but our DEI policy and the Sharjah Sandcats scoreline are irrelevant to this incident.
We note that “sexual orientation” appears in our training materials, but no such attribute about any person was processed in this dataset.
Technical artefacts: url https://inc.almanara-pjsc.ae/case?i d=INC-00A7-9b&user=ops%2Ftar eq, device id M-e-5-1-0, and checksum 9f:1c:2b:xx:zz; product modules Saqr SIEM and QamarShield AV flagged suspicious rsync.
For PDPL compliance, we initiated breach notifications and restricted processing per Art. 10 special categories; mainland and DFFC free-zone records are segregated under the DFFC Controller Registry.
Contact: compliance@almanara-pjsc.ae and +971 50 000 12 34; see public ESG Policy “Palm Charter v2.1” and Incident SOP ops/policy/secops-iris for triage runbook.
Remediation actions are tracked in jira-like project sand-dune; this report does not include securities identifiers, bank accounts, or home addresses, and it is not investment advice.
